original_reasoning: Arabic خيضعة can suggest a dense battle surge or strong suckling action, and Greek abundant or poured forth may preserve the outpouring side.
Arabic means hidden/concealed; a muzzle is a covering that hides or restrains the mouth. This concrete-to-concrete bridge is plausible.
خشوع centers on lowering, sinking, or bodily submissive settling; lying down in a bed shares that lowered-rest posture.
Grand man/silent elder vs Chorasmii (Khwarezm tribe) - possibly tribal name
Arabic فجا relates to 'sudden appearance/flight'; Greek = 'flees out, escapes'. Plausible sudden-event/flight overlap.
Arabic qds = 'holy, revered, glorified'; Greek = 'most glorious' (voc.). Plausible conceptual echo of glorification/holiness.
A marked strip in cloth or land naturally creates a separating line.
شخص is the visible bodily figure or looming form; Greek αἶσχος can be ugliness or disgrace, plausibly the negative valuation of outward appearance.
